cases.passage: In the bustling city of Mumbai, there exists a variety of small-scale retail shops that cater to the daily needs of the local population. One such shop is 'Ramesh Stores', which is owned by Ramesh, an entrepreneur who has been in the business for over a decade. Ramesh offers a wide range of products, including groceries, stationery, and household items. However, he has been facing stiff competition from larger retail chains that have set up shop in the city. To stay competitive, Ramesh has decided to adopt a strategy of offering discounts and promotions to his customers. He has also set up a loyalty program to reward his regular customers. Ramesh's main objective is to increase his sales and maintain a loyal customer base.

questions.q: Why did Ramesh decide to adopt a strategy of offering discounts and promotions?
opts: (B) To stay competitive in the market
questions.explanation: Ramesh decided to adopt a strategy of offering discounts and promotions to stay competitive in the market, as larger retail chains were also offering similar deals to their customers.

questions.q: What is the main objective of Ramesh's loyalty program?
opts: (C) To maintain a loyal customer base
questions.explanation: The main objective of Ramesh's loyalty program is to maintain a loyal customer base, as it rewards his regular customers and encourages them to continue shopping at his store.

questions.q: What is the name of Ramesh's store?
questions.explanation: The name of Ramesh's store is 'Ramesh Stores'.

questions.q: What type of products does Ramesh's store offer?
opts: (D) A wide range of products including groceries, stationery, and household items
questions.explanation: Ramesh's store offers a wide range of products including groceries, stationery, and household items.

questions.q: Why is Ramesh's store facing stiff competition?
opts: (B) Due to his low quality products
(C) Due to the presence of larger retail chains
(D) Due to his poor customer service
questions.explanation: Ramesh's store is facing stiff competition due to the presence of larger retail chains that have set up shop in the city.

cases.passage: Savita, a small-scale retailer, owns a shop in a local market in Delhi. She sells a variety of cosmetics and personal care products. Savita has been facing difficulties in maintaining a steady supply of her products due to the unreliable transportation system and high costs associated with it. She has been considering partnering with a local wholesaler to improve her supply chain. However, she is unsure about the terms and conditions of the partnership and is worried about losing control over her business.

questions.q: Why is Savita facing difficulties in maintaining a steady supply of her products?
questions.explanation: Savita is facing difficulties in maintaining a steady supply of her products due to the unreliable transportation system.

questions.q: What is Savita considering to improve her supply chain?
opts: (B) Investing in her own transportation system
questions.explanation: Savita is considering partnering with a local wholesaler to improve her supply chain.

questions.q: What is Savita worried about in terms of partnering with a local wholesaler?
questions.explanation: Savita is worried about losing control over her business if she partners with a local wholesaler.

questions.q: What type of products does Savita sell in her shop?
opts: (C) Only cosmetics and personal care products
questions.explanation: Savita sells a variety of cosmetics and personal care products in her shop.

questions.q: Why is Savita considering partnering with a local wholesaler?
questions.explanation: Savita is considering partnering with a local wholesaler to improve her supply chain.
